chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C. P No. D-139 of 2026 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For order on office objection. 2. For hearing of M.A No.611/2026 (S/A). 3. For hearing of main case. 03-03-2026 Mr. Atta Hussain Qadri, advocate a/w petitioners. Mr. Liaquat Ali Shar, A.A.G a/w Inspector Shahzado, of PS Suhrab Goth Karachi. Mr. Nazir Ahmed Bhangwar, D.P.G for the State..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Respondent No.2 is present and submits that the Investigating Officer of Crime No. 30/2026 is engaged before the Circuit Court of the High Court at Hyderabad. He has filed a progress report along with statement, which are taken on record. The report reflects that an interim challan has been submitted before the concerned Magistrate. It is further submitted that today the statement of petitioner No.1 has been recorded, wherein she has categorically stated that she has contracted marriage with petitioner No.2 of her free will and has not been abducted. The concerned S.H.O submits that the said statement shall be handed over to the Investigating Officer for placing it before the competent forum in accordance with law. Respondent No.5 (father of petitioner No.1) is present in person and submits that petitioner No.1 has been abducted and is under age. Learned Deputy Prosecutor General submits that the official respondents shall act strictly in accordance with law and shall provide protection to the petitioners, if and when required. Learned counsel for the petitioners, after going through the progress report and statements, seeks disposal of the petition. However, he requests that directions be issued to the official respondents to provide protection to the petitioners and that the statement of petitioner No.1 be placed before the concerned Magistrate.

Learned counsel for the petitioners, after going through the progress report and statements, seeks disposal of the petition. However, he requests that directions be issued to the official respondents to provide protection to the petitioners and that the statement of petitioner No.1 be placed before the concerned Magistrate. In view of the submissions made and the material placed on record, this petition is disposed of with the direction that the official respondents shall ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioners and shall provide protection to them, as and when required, strictly in accordance with law. The Investigating Officer shall proceed in accordance with law and place the statement of petitioner No.1 along with police report, if any, before the concerned Magistrate, who shall examine the same and pass appropriate orders independently, strictly in accordance with law and without being influenced by any observation made herein. So far as the contention of respondent No.5 regarding the alleged minority of petitioner No.1 is concerned, he shall be at liberty to avail any remedy available to him under the law before the competent forum having jurisdiction. Judge Judge Irshad Ali M/Steno
